It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Properties]]. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:Takanabe Station|Takanabe Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Coord|32.14222222222222|N|131.52972222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Takanabe|WD=Q1206737}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|08|32|N|131|31|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Mochida Kofun cluster'''|持田古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mochida-cho neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206823|title= 持田古墳群|trans-title= Mochida Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mochida Kofun Cluster consists of ten {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 75 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs located on the downstream left bank of the Komaru River, which runs through the center of the prefecture. The Kofuns are on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 50 meters, extending from east-to-west, bordering {{ill|Japan National Route 10|en|Japan National Route 10|WD=Q873337|lt=Japan National Route 10}}, and on an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain}} that spreads out to the southeast, but most of the Kofuns are concentrated on the west side of the plateau.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mochida No. 1, also known as the "Hakarizuka Kofun", located on the southwest edge of the flat plateau, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of approximately 100 meters and is the oldest and largest of the group. It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Mochida Kofun Cluster}} *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_mochida.html Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.takanabe.lg.jp/soshiki/shakaikyoiku/4/2/3279.html Takanabe town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kankou-takanabe.com/siteseeing/kofun Takanabe Tourist Association] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The {{nihongo|'''Mochida Kofun cluster'''|持田古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mochida-cho neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206823|title= 持田古墳群|trans-title= Mochida Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mochida Kofun Cluster consists of ten {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 75 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs located on the downstream left bank of the Komaru River, which runs through the center of the prefecture. The Kofuns are on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 50 meters, extending from east-to-west, bordering {{ill|Japan National Route 10|en|Japan National Route 10|WD=Q873337|lt=Japan National Route 10}}, and on an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain}} that spreads out to the southeast, but most of the Kofuns are concentrated on the west side of the plateau.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mochida No. 1, also known as the "Hakarizuka Kofun", located on the southwest edge of the flat plateau, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of approximately 100 meters and is the oldest and largest of the group. It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties}}. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Mochida Kofun Cluster}} *[http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mk-heritage/burialmounds/summary_mochida.html Miyazaki prefecture] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.takanabe.lg.jp/soshiki/shakaikyoiku/4/2/3279.html Takanabe town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kankou-takanabe.com/siteseeing/kofun Takanabe Tourist Association] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Mochida Kofun cluster'''|持田古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mochida-cho neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe|1=Takanabe, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206823|title= 持田古墳群|trans-title= Mochida Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mochida Kofun Cluster consists of ten {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 75 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs located on the downstream left bank of the Komaru River, which runs through the center of the prefecture. The Kofuns are on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 50 meters, extending from east-to-west, bordering {{ill|Japan National Route 10|en|Japan National Route 10|WD=Q873337|lt=Japan National Route 10|1=Japan National Route 10}}, and on an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}} that spreads out to the southeast, but most of the Kofuns are concentrated on the west side of the plateau.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mochida No. 1, also known as the "Hakarizuka Kofun", located on the southwest edge of the flat plateau, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of approximately 100 meters and is the oldest and largest of the group. It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Mochida No. 1, also known as the "Hakarizuka Kofun", located on the southwest edge of the flat plateau, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of approximately 100 meters and is the oldest and largest of the group. It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|WD=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206823|title= 持田古墳群|trans-title= Mochida Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>

The Kofuns are on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 50 meters, extending from east-to-west, bordering {{ill|Japan National Route 10|en|Japan National Route 10|WD=Q873337|lt=Japan National Route 10|1=Japan National Route 10}}, and on an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|WD=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}} that spreads out to the southeast, but most of the Kofuns are concentrated on the west side of the plateau.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|WD=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|qid=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mochida Kofun Cluster]][[en:Mochida Kofun Cluster]][[ja:持田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mochida Kofun Cluster]][[en:Mochida Kofun Cluster]][[ja:持田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mochida Kofun Cluster]][[ja:持田古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Mochida Kofun cluster | native_name =持田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =持田26号墳出土 三葉環頭大刀・小刀.JPG|image= File:Mochida No.15 Ishifunezuka.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Three leaf ring-headed swords and short swords excavated from Mochida Tomb No. 26 | map_type = Japan Miyazaki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mochida Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|08|32|N|131|31|47|E|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.14222222222222|N|131.52972222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|qid=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe, Miyazaki|1=Takanabe, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Takanabe|qid=Q1206737|1=Takanabe, Miyazaki}}| region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =c. 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No facilities | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|08|32|N|131|31|47|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Mochida Kofun cluster'''|持田古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Mochida-cho neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en|Takanabe, Miyazaki|qid=Q1206737|lt=Takanabe|1=Takanabe, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} in [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus group was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name= "Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206823|title= 持田古墳群|trans-title= Mochida Kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=January 1, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mochida Kofun Cluster consists of ten {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, and 75 {{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tombs located on the downstream left bank of the Komaru River, which runs through the center of the prefecture. The Kofuns are on a plateau at an elevation of approximately 50 meters, extending from east-to-west, bordering {{ill|Japan National Route 10|en|Japan National Route 10|qid=Q873337|lt=Japan National Route 10|1=Japan National Route 10}}, and on an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|qid=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}} that spreads out to the southeast, but most of the Kofuns are concentrated on the west side of the plateau.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Mochida No. 1, also known as the "Hakarizuka Kofun", located on the southwest edge of the flat plateau, is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of approximately 100 meters and is the oldest and largest of the group. It was built in the first half of the 5th century. Kofun building reached its peak from the late 5th century to the beginning of the 6th century. Of particular note are the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} which included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, and 34 of which have been designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The bronze mirrors from Mochida Kofun No. 20, 24 and 25 are identical in design to a bronze mirror found in the Gokantenjinyama Kofun in [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]]. Other valuable artifacts include a large amount of beads and a ring-headed iron sword. These grave goods are strongly associated with the [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai region]], and are thought to indicate that the area served as an important base for the expansion of the Yamato Kingdom within eastern Kyushu. Around 1929 and 1930, the area suffered from extensive tomb robbery, and as a result, many grave goods were scattered into private collections or have otherwise become lost.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Mochida Kofun Cluster is approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Takanabe Station|en|Takanabe Station|qid=Q5357588|lt=Takanabe Station|1=Takanabe Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|qid=Q6573277|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyazaki)}}
